overall_justification: While the article mentions arguments from both sides, the overall tone and framing lean towards the Orthodox perspective. The emphasis on potential dangers and difficulties, combined with the criticism of the Convênio de Taubaté's approach, suggests a cautious, conservative stance more aligned with Orthodox views.  The expansionist arguments are presented but are countered or undermined by the newspaper's commentary.

supporting_evidence.quote: A Companhia Dumont [...] ataca o projecto de valorisação de café [...] e ataca tambem o projecto de fixação do cambio, dizendo que «o projecto dessa remissão de papel parece conter muitos elementos de perigo e pode lançar o paiz em difficuldades mais graves do que actualmente»
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote highlights the newspaper's focus on the potential negative consequences of the Caixa de Conversão, a key concern of the Orthodox perspective. It also connects the criticism to a specific entity, giving it more weight.

supporting_evidence.quote: De modo que se a valorisação podesse ser levada a effeito, o Estado de S. Paulo estaria livre da necessidade de intervir perante a grande massa da producção das fazendas Dumont, que preferiria vender-se aos preços correntes
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote subtly undermines the expansionist argument by suggesting that the valorization scheme, often linked to the Caixa, would not be as effective as proponents claim.

supporting_evidence.quote: Mas ha outros milagres, na miraculosa historia da Companhia Dumont [...].  Não houve augmento de preço, todos o sabem; de sorte que o caso miraculoso da Companhia Dumont [...] só pode ser attribuido a reducções no custo de producção.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote further reinforces the Orthodox skepticism towards government intervention, suggesting that real improvements come from cost reductions, not artificial price supports.
